summary: Fix restart invocation to unwind the stack (running interposing unwind-protect cleanups) BEFORE evaluating the chosen restart clause, per CLHS.

items.title: Defer restart handler call until after non-local unwind
items.description:
Per CLHS INVOKE-RESTART, the non-local transfer of control must complete (running every interposing UNWIND-PROTECT cleanup) BEFORE the restart's function is called. Today src/core/builtins_condition.c:invoke_restart_at_binding does the opposite — it calls cl_vm_apply(handler, args) FIRST, then cl_throw_to_tag(tag, result). This makes cleanups run AFTER the handler. The 8-line repro in the request, and equivalently /workspace/restart_uwp_repro.lisp, confirms the wrong order on host (currently prints `before HANDLER CLEANUP`; CL requires `before CLEANUP HANDLER`).

Fix strategy — preserve the existing one-catch-per-restart-case design; defer the handler call to the catch landing:

1) src/core/builtins_condition.c (invoke_restart_at_binding, ~line 1230): Build a Lisp list of call_args, then build a `(handler . args-list)` cons (CL_GC_PROTECT the args-list while consing). Read handler/tag from cl_restart_stack[i] AFTER all allocations (they are GC-rooted in the thread struct, so re-reading the fields is safe and dodges stale C locals after compaction). Throw the cons as the value: `cl_throw_to_tag(tag, dispatch_cons)`. Do NOT call cl_vm_apply on the handler here. This is the change that makes interposing UNWIND-PROTECT cleanups (and OP_UWRETHROW chains via cl_pending_value, which is already GC-marked in src/core/mem.c:1209) run before the handler.

2) src/core/compiler_special.c (compile_restart_case, ~line 2272): Allocate one anonymous local slot in env->locals BEFORE compiling main_form (same pattern as compile_unwind_protect at lines 1193–1198: bump env->local_count, update env->max_locals, set env->locals[slot]=CL_NIL). After the existing `cl_patch_jump(c, catch_pos);` and BEFORE `cl_patch_jump(c, jmp_pos);`, emit a dispatch sequence that turns the thrown `(handler . args)` cons on TOS into a handler-applied result:
     OP_DUP
     OP_CDR              ; TOS = args, below = cons
     OP_STORE slot       ; OP_STORE peeks (does not pop) — see vm.c VM_CASE(OP_STORE)
     OP_POP              ; drop args copy
     OP_CAR              ; TOS = handler, cons replaced
     OP_LOAD slot        ; push args
     OP_APPLY            ; pops args+fn (vm.c VM_CASE(OP_APPLY)), pushes result
   Then fall through to `cl_patch_jump(c, jmp_pos);` so both paths converge with one value on the stack. Restore env->local_count after emission (the slot is only live in the landing path, but max_locals reservation keeps the stack frame sized correctly). The handler now executes in the dynamic environment captured at OP_CATCH (dyn_mark / handler_mark / restart_mark are already restored by the existing OP_CATCH longjmp path in vm.c lines 3229–3257), matching CLHS: 'in the dynamic environment which existed at the point of the restart's establishment.'

3) src/core/fasl.h: bump CL_FASL_VERSION 10 → 11 with a trailing comment 'v11: restart-case landing applies handler post-unwind (cons-dispatch sequence)'. Required because compile_restart_case emits a different bytecode tail, so stale FASLs must be rejected/recompiled — failing to bump would silently mis-execute restart-case in cached FASLs (CLAUDE.md FASL Versioning Critical). After bumping, the build/test step `make fasl && make test` regenerates bundled FASLs.

4) Regression tests — exact behavior, not just happy-path:
   - tests/test_condition.c: add `lisp_restart_case_runs_uwp_cleanup_before_handler` mirroring the repro: bind *log* with (let ((*log* nil)) ...) then (restart-case (unwind-protect (progn (push 'before *log*) (invoke-restart 'abort)) (push 'cleanup *log*)) (abort () (push 'handler *log*) :done)) and ASSERT_STR_EQ the reversed list equals `(BEFORE CLEANUP HANDLER)`. Also add a nested-UWP test: two unwind-protects nested, verify cleanups run innermost-first, both before the handler. Also add a test that the handler's return value is the value of restart-case (handler returns :done).
   - tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp: add the same two checks in the existing condition/restart section so the Amiga suite covers the fix on real hardware.
   - Re-run pre-existing restart tests in test_condition.c (lisp_restart_case_basic, lisp_break_with_continue, lisp_handler_case_t_catches_secondary_error_from_invoke_restart, etc.) — they should all still pass because the externally observable result and value flow are unchanged when no UNWIND-PROTECT interposes.

GC-safety checklist for this change:
   - The dispatch cons and args-list built in invoke_restart_at_binding are reached only via the throw value, which ends up in nlx->result (GC-marked, src/core/mem.c:1170) and/or cl_pending_value (GC-marked, src/core/mem.c:1209). CL_GC_PROTECT the args-list local while looping cl_cons over call_args[].
   - cl_restart_stack[i].handler/tag fields are GC-marked AND gc_update_slot'd (src/core/mem.c:1182–1185 and 1902–1905), so re-reading them after allocations is safe.
   - Allocated slot in compile_restart_case is initialized to CL_NIL before main_form compiles (same pattern as compile_unwind_protect) to avoid stale bindings.

Validation: rebuild host (`make host`), run `./build/host/clamiga --load restart_uwp_repro.lisp` and confirm log prints `(BEFORE CLEANUP HANDLER)`; run `make test`; regenerate FASLs (`make fasl`); cross-compile and run Amiga suite (`make -f Makefile.cross test-amiga`).
